user_message: Help me prep the migration architecture for Litware Inc. This is time-sensitive -- customer is waiting.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-08 17:28 EST
Subject: URGENT: migration architecture Issue at Litware Inc

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about migration architecture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
Date: 2024-02-19 10:55 EST
Subject: migration architecture - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about migration architecture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-23 16:40 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Litware Inc migration architecture

Our team encountered some challenges with migration architecture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: migration architecture Discussion - Litware Inc
Date: 2024-02-04
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc), David Chen (Microsoft), Priya (Litware Inc)

[00:01:30] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into migration architecture.

[00:08:00] Priya (Litware Inc): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:10:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about partner engagement. Can we address those?

[00:12:00] Priya (Litware Inc): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:16:00] David Chen (Microsoft): The timeline for data residency is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:19:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around budget approval.

[00:22:00] David Chen (Microsoft): The timeline for technical validation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:25:00] Priya (Litware Inc): The timeline for cost optimization is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:29:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:33:00] Priya (Litware Inc): Let me add some context -- we've been working on security compliance and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:37:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Litware Inc: the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades

Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence
